title: Indexer Queue Fill Ratio (parsing/aggqueue/typingqueue/indexqueue saturation, blocked-queue dwell, throughput burn-rate, restart-aware backlog)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.1.23 disk fullness analytics, UC-13.1.10 search head cluster health, universal forwarder version compliance programs in isolation, or network perimeter analytics; does not automatically resize indexers or mutate server.conf; produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved capacity actions.
evidence: Saved search uc_13_1_1_indexer_queue_saturation with five-minute schedule, Monitoring Console drilldowns, weekly CSV exports of alert results to a restricted platform_evidence index, and splunk_indexer_inventory.csv version hash archived with change tickets.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab indexer, induce sustained parsingQueue depth by temporarily installing an expensive props.conf regex on a high-volume sourcetype, confirm uc_13_1_1_indexer_queue_saturation returns warn or page with non-null worst_queue and blocked_dwell_sec within ten minutes while restart_hint stays zero.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During an approved rolling restart with documented maintenance metadata, confirm restart_hint turns positive, page-tier severities suppress for hosts inside the restart window, and severity returns to info after queues drain without manual alert suppression.

dataSources: index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd with group=queue for parsingQueue aggQueue typingQueue indexQueue current and max sizes; group=queue_thruput for per-queue kbps in and out; group=pipelineinputchannel for parallel pipeline channel pressure; group=tcpin_connections for forwarder connection load; index=_audit for splunkd_started and splunkd_stopped style lifecycle rows used to mask restart backlog; optional Monitoring Console distilled summaries and optional splunk_metrics mstats when customers pre-aggregate queue KPIs; lookup splunk_indexer_inventory.csv enriches cluster tier, owner team, and environment by cluster key.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.1.1 indexer pipeline saturation: q_fill q_thru pl_chan tcpin restarts mask. Tunables earliest=-4h@h latest=now fill_warn=0.80 fill_page=0.92 dwell_page_sec=300 tcpin_warn=5000")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=queue earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, splunk_server, host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval qname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, queue, ""))))
      | where match(qname,"parsingqueue|aggqueue|typingqueue|indexqueue")
      | eval cur_sz=tonumber(coalesce(current_size_kb, current_size, 0))
      | eval max_sz=tonumber(coalesce(max_size_kb, max_size, 1))
      | eval fill_ratio=if(max_sz>0, round(cur_sz/max_sz,4), null())
      | eval blocked_q=if(fill_ratio>=0.85,1,0)
      | eval lane="q_fill"
      | fields _time cluster host qname fill_ratio blocked_q lane cur_sz max_sz ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=queue_thruput earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, splunk_server, host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval qname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, queue, ""))))
      | eval kbps_in=tonumber(coalesce(kbps_in, kb_in, 0))
      | eval kbps_out=tonumber(coalesce(kbps_out, kb_out, 0))
      | eval lane="q_thru"
      | fields _time cluster host qname kbps_in kbps_out lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=pipelineinputchannel earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, splunk_server, host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval chan=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(channel, pipeline_id, name, "parallel"))))
      | eval bytes_in=tonumber(coalesce(current_size_kb, current_size, 0))
      | eval lane="pl_chan"
      | fields _time cluster host chan bytes_in lane qname ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=tcpin_connections earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, splunk_server, host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval fwd_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(connections, connection_count, fwd_count, 0))
      | eval lane="tcpin"
      | eval qname="tcpin_agg"
      | fields _time cluster host fwd_cnt lane qname ]
    [ search index=_audit earliest=-4h@h latest=now (action=splunkd_started OR action=splunkd_stopped)
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval lane="restarts"
      | eval restart_window=1
      | eval qname="restart_evt"
      | fields _time cluster host lane restart_window qname ]
| eval qname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(qname, "na"))))
| fillnull value=0 blocked_q fill_ratio kbps_in kbps_out fwd_cnt bytes_in restart_window
| sort 0 + cluster host qname - _time
| streamstats window=10 current=t global=f last(_time) AS prev_ts last(blocked_q) AS prev_blk BY cluster host qname
| eval blocked_dwell_sec=if(blocked_q==1 AND prev_blk==1 AND isnotnull(prev_ts), if(_time-prev_ts>0,_time-prev_ts,0), if(blocked_q==1,0,0))
| eventstats sum(eval(if(lane=="restarts",restart_window,0))) AS cluster_restart_hint sum(eval(if(lane=="tcpin",fwd_cnt,0))) AS cluster_tcpin_sum max(eval(if(lane=="q_fill",fill_ratio,0))) AS cluster_peak_fill BY cluster
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen
        max(eval(if(lane=="q_fill",fill_ratio,null()))) AS fill_ratio
        max(eval(if(lane=="q_fill",blocked_dwell_sec,null()))) AS blocked_dwell_sec
        max(eval(if(lane=="q_thru",kbps_in,null()))) AS kbps_in
        max(eval(if(lane=="q_thru",kbps_out,null()))) AS kbps_out
        max(eval(if(lane=="tcpin",fwd_cnt,null()))) AS tcpin_connections
        max(eval(if(lane=="pl_chan",bytes_in,null()))) AS pipeline_chan_kb
        max(eval(if(lane=="restarts",restart_window,null()))) AS restart_hint
        values(eval(if(lane=="q_fill",qname,null()))) AS qname_vals
        values(lane) AS lane_vals
  BY cluster host
| eval worst_queue=mvindex(mvdedup(qname_vals),0)
| eval source_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lane_vals),0)
| eval burn_rate=if(kbps_out>0 AND fill_ratio>0, round(kbps_out/(fill_ratio+0.0001),2), null())
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(fill_ratio,0)>=0.92 AND coalesce(restart_hint,0)<1, "page",
    coalesce(blocked_dwell_sec,0)>300 AND coalesce(restart_hint,0)<1 AND coalesce(fill_ratio,0)>=0.85, "page",
    coalesce(fill_ratio,0)>=0.80, "warn",
    coalesce(tcpin_connections,0)>=5000, "warn",
    coalesce(pipeline_chan_kb,0)>=1000000, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| join type=left max=0 cluster
    [| inputlookup splunk_indexer_inventory.csv
     | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
     | dedup cluster
     | fields cluster, cluster_tier, owner_team, environment ]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier, "unrated"), owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, "unowned"), environment=coalesce(environment, "unknown")
| table cluster host worst_queue fill_ratio blocked_dwell_sec kbps_in kbps_out burn_rate tcpin_connections pipeline_chan_kb restart_hint severity last_seen source_lane cluster_tier owner_team environment
description: Detects indexer-side splunkd pipeline saturation by tracking parsingQueue, aggQueue, typingQueue, and indexQueue fill ratio over time, consecutive blocked-queue dwell, queue_thruput burn-rate context, pipelineinputchannel imbalance signals, tcpin forwarder reconnect pressure, and restart-masked backlog using _internal metrics.log families plus _audit lifecycle hints joined to inventory metadata.
value: Platform leaders shorten mean time to innocence when search feels slow but the real problem is ingestion backlog: this UC gives a tamper-resistant ledger that ties queue depth, throughput movement, parallel pipeline pressure, connection storms, and restart windows into one analyst-facing row per indexer so capacity reviews fund the right fixes—disk, parsing cost, forwarder behavior, or knowledge bundle churn—instead of guessing.
implementation: Schedule uc_13_1_1_indexer_queue_saturation every five minutes from a search head or Monitoring Console with earliest=-4h@h, keep splunk_indexer_inventory.csv current for cluster tier and ownership, tune fill and dwell thresholds per cluster class, route page rows to indexer on-call with MC and metrics.log drilldowns, and archive weekly exports of alert evidence for capacity reviews.
visualization: Time-series overlay of fill_ratio and kbps_out per worst_queue, single-value tiles for restart_hint and tcpin_connections, severity-colored table by cluster and host with drilldowns to metrics.log slices, paired Monitoring Console queue panels, and optional Performance model roll-ups when accelerated.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries when you map queue-derived host metrics into Performance objects for fleet roll-ups; keep fourteen days hot for incident replay and ninety days for quarterly capacity attestations.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Work with metrics.log files
Splunk Docs — Understand and troubleshoot indexing performance
Splunk Docs — Introduction to capacity planning
Splunk Lantern — Platform administration and capacity
Splunk Docs — Splunk Enterprise system requirements

knownFalsePositives: Planned ingestion surges during marketing events or batch replays can park bytes in parsingQueue and typingQueue without a broken disk; pair peaks with change calendars and forwarder fan-in dashboards before you treat the cluster as sick. Summary-index and report-acceleration bursts inflate aggQueue because the indexer is doing exactly what you asked on a tight schedule; shift heavy summaries or widen search windows instead of paging like it is a mystery outage. Replication catch-up after a peer rejoin often deepens indexQueue while buckets reconcile; when tcpin is calm and disk latency is normal, let the cluster breathe and watch dwell decay rather than opening a sev-1. Mid-restart windows frequently show indexQueue backlog while pipelines reconnect; the restarts arm exists precisely so those minutes do not become false tragedies. Hot-bucket rolls and cold-path transitions can flicker queue depth for seconds; require sustained dwell beyond roll timing or correlate with bucket path errors. Search-head bundle pushes can transiently starve typing throughput even though ingestion volume is unchanged; cross-check shcluster artifact traffic before you rip out network gear. Compression or SSL renegotiation storms on tcpin can look like saturation in the connection lane while queues remain modest; validate universal forwarder settings and load balancer idle timeouts. Executive dashboards that only glance at fill ratio miss the story when pipelineinputchannel imbalance hides a single hot parallel lane; keep the channel lane attached so false calm does not survive a leadership review.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the Splunk operations team that runs indexer clusters, cluster managers, and the Monitoring Console, plus the observability engineers who maintain fleet-wide dashboards and alert routing. This use case isolates indexer-side pipeline saturation inside splunkd: parsingQueue, aggQueue, typingQueue, and indexQueue depth expressed as fill ratio against each queue max, blocked-queue dwell derived from consecutive high-fill samples, throughput burn-rate context from queue_thruput, parallel pipeline pressure from pipelineinputchannel, forwarder reconnect pressure from tcpin_connections, and restart masking from audit-style lifecycle rows so a healthy post-restart backlog does not page the same way as steady-state starvation. The narrative intentionally complements rather than replaces sibling platform-health UCs: UC-13.1.23 remains authoritative for disk space, mount fullness, and frozen path pressure where the bottleneck is bytes on volume rather than bytes waiting in RAM queues; UC-13.1.10 remains search head cluster captain elections, artifact replication, and user-visible search capacity rather than ingestion-side queueing; this UC answers whether splunkd is out of pipeline headroom on a peer before executives blame the wrong tier.

HTTP Event Collector tokens do not participate in this signal path. Queue depth arrives through metrics.log lines emitted locally by splunkd on each indexer, indexed into index=_internal with sourcetype=splunkd, grouped by metrics.log group keys such as queue, queue_thruput, pipelineinputchannel, and tcpin_connections. Dispatch the saved search from a search head or from the Monitoring Console search head tier so Job Inspector remains available to operators; running interactively on an indexer is possible for break-glass triage but duplicates load on the peer you are diagnosing. Namespace conventions for correlation: cluster carries the cluster manager identifier or a curator-approved pseudo-cluster label that matches splunk_indexer_inventory.csv; host carries the indexer hostname or server name you expect in service discovery; name carries the queue or channel identifier inside each metrics group; group carries the metrics.log group discriminator. Platform RBAC should grant authors read access to _internal and _audit on indexers, read access to the knowledge objects hosting splunk_indexer_inventory.csv, and alert manager rights on the destination app. Restrict sharing of raw _internal exports outside the platform team because forwarder metadata and file paths can surface sensitive workload hints.

Field naming drift across Splunk versions makes naive arithmetic brittle. Some builds expose current_size_kb and max_size_kb for queue rows; others expose current_size and max_size without the _kb suffix; a few lab builds interleave both depending on the metrics channel. Coalesce ladders in the SPL normalize size before fill_ratio math so upgrades do not silence the alert through empty evals. Treat a sudden flatline at zero as a collection problem: verify metrics.log rotation, ulimit issues, or a paused input before celebrating an empty queue. Parallel ingestion pipelines change the meaning of saturation: pipelineinputchannel rows reveal per-pipeline pressure that a single aggregate queue metric might average away, so keep that lane online even when queue fill looks polite.

Risk briefing: rolling restarts and maintenance windows create legitimate bursts where queues deepen while pipelines drain; without restart awareness, any transient fill spike becomes a midnight page storm. Bundle pushes from search head clusters can depress effective throughput on indexers during short windows; correlate with shcluster status UCs before declaring ingestion collapse. Large summary search bursts can elevate aggQueue and typingQueue through transform cost even when raw tcpin volume looks stable. Replication catch-up after a peer rejoin can elevate indexQueue while buckets reconcile; distinguish that pattern from slow disks by pairing with disk latency and replication channel metrics in adjacent runbooks. Paging purely on fill ratio without dwell burns goodwill; require sustained blocked-queue dwell or extreme fill with restart_hint at zero for page-tier routing.

Differentiation recap: Splunk-internal ingestion queues and their throughput mirrors, not generic Linux CPU, not search head clustering, not indexer filesystem fullness in isolation, not universal forwarder version compliance without queue context.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up six complementary lanes the multisearch arms consume plus an optional seventh when your estate already lands normalized queue KPIs into a metrics index.

Lane A, group=queue: ensure every indexer forwards or locally indexes metrics.log lines where group=queue and name matches parsingQueue, aggQueue, typingQueue, and indexQueue. Validate that current_size and max_size fields appear on a five-minute cadence during steady load. For clustered indexers, confirm each peer emits its own host value so per-peer isolation remains possible. If you operate heavy single-node lab hosts, still keep host granularity so future cluster migrations do not require parser rewrites.

Lane B, group=queue_thruput: capture kbps in and out per queue name. This lane fuels burn-rate style reasoning when fill grows despite outward movement, or when outward movement stalls while fill remains high. Align clocks across peers; skewed NTP makes rate deltas deceptive when you correlate with external ingestion audits.

Lane C, group=pipelineinputchannel: capture parallel pipeline channel depth or byte pressure fields your version exposes. Use this lane to detect imbalance where one pipeline saturates while aggregate queue metrics mask the hotspot. When pipeline counts change after parallelIngestionPipelines adjustments, update dashboard annotations so operators know expected cardinality shifts.

Lane D, group=tcpin_connections: capture connection counts or equivalent forwarder session load indicators your build exposes. Pair with connection quality metrics from the same family when available so you can tell a reconnect storm from a steady high fan-in design.

Lane E, index=_audit lifecycle: land splunkd_started and splunkd_stopped style actions if your audit configuration emits them; where your deployment maps lifecycle signals to a different index or sourcetype, mirror those events into the restarts arm with consistent cluster and host keys. The goal is a boolean-friendly restart_hint per window, not a perfect forensic audit trail.

Lane F optional, metrics store: when you pre-aggregate queue fill into index=splunk_metrics or another metrics index via collect or Edge Processor, add an mstats arm later behind a macro toggle. Keep this UC’s primary implementation on _internal so greenfield sites are not forced to stand up metrics schema first.

Supporting inventory: maintain splunk_indexer_inventory.csv with columns cluster, cluster_tier, owner_team, and environment at minimum. Cluster must match the cluster field produced by the SPL coalesce ladder; if you have not yet threaded cluster manager labels into metrics, derive cluster from CMDB lookup at ingest time using transforms rather than muting the join. Refresh the CSV weekly or automate KV refresh so ownership pages route to the correct squad.

Monitoring Console posture: enable distributed mode, confirm indexer discovery, and verify the queue dashboards show the same peaks your raw search surfaces. Splunk_TA_nix remains valuable for host CPU, memory, and disk wait correlation even though this UC’s primary signal is splunkd metrics.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_1_1_indexer_queue_saturation with a five-minute schedule for production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and throttle duplicate cluster host rows for twenty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Route page severities to the indexer on-call; route warn severities to a platform chat channel with Job Inspector links. Keep a clone scheduled every hour for trend dashboards with higher earliest windows to reduce noise.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms—q_fill, q_thru, pl_chan, tcpin, and restarts—so a silent single lane does not hide a real incident. coalesce() ladders absorb current_size_kb versus current_size drift. streamstats measures consecutive blocked-queue dwell for parsing and merging families without expensive transaction commands. eventstats adds cluster_restart_hint so benign backlog right after splunkd bounce does not page. case() maps sustained dwell and peak fill into page versus warn versus info tiers with tcpin and pipeline channel guardrails. join enriches rows from splunk_indexer_inventory.csv for pager context. The closing table preserves analyst-ready columns for drilldowns into worst_queue, burn_rate, tcpin_connections, and restart_hint.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.1 indexer pipeline saturation: q_fill q_thru pl_chan tcpin restarts mask. Tunables earliest=-4h@h latest=now fill_warn=0.80 fill_page=0.92 dwell_page_sec=300 tcpin_warn=5000")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=queue earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, splunk_server, host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval qname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, queue, ""))))
      | where match(qname,"parsingqueue|aggqueue|typingqueue|indexqueue")
      | eval cur_sz=tonumber(coalesce(current_size_kb, current_size, 0))
      | eval max_sz=tonumber(coalesce(max_size_kb, max_size, 1))
      | eval fill_ratio=if(max_sz>0, round(cur_sz/max_sz,4), null())
      | eval blocked_q=if(fill_ratio>=0.85,1,0)
      | eval lane="q_fill"
      | fields _time cluster host qname fill_ratio blocked_q lane cur_sz max_sz ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=queue_thruput earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, splunk_server, host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval qname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, queue, ""))))
      | eval kbps_in=tonumber(coalesce(kbps_in, kb_in, 0))
      | eval kbps_out=tonumber(coalesce(kbps_out, kb_out, 0))
      | eval lane="q_thru"
      | fields _time cluster host qname kbps_in kbps_out lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=pipelineinputchannel earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, splunk_server, host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval chan=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(channel, pipeline_id, name, "parallel"))))
      | eval bytes_in=tonumber(coalesce(current_size_kb, current_size, 0))
      | eval lane="pl_chan"
      | fields _time cluster host chan bytes_in lane qname ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=tcpin_connections earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, splunk_server, host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval fwd_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(connections, connection_count, fwd_count, 0))
      | eval lane="tcpin"
      | eval qname="tcpin_agg"
      | fields _time cluster host fwd_cnt lane qname ]
    [ search index=_audit earliest=-4h@h latest=now (action=splunkd_started OR action=splunkd_stopped)
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster, host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval lane="restarts"
      | eval restart_window=1
      | eval qname="restart_evt"
      | fields _time cluster host lane restart_window qname ]
| eval qname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(qname, "na"))))
| fillnull value=0 blocked_q fill_ratio kbps_in kbps_out fwd_cnt bytes_in restart_window
| sort 0 + cluster host qname - _time
| streamstats window=10 current=t global=f last(_time) AS prev_ts last(blocked_q) AS prev_blk BY cluster host qname
| eval blocked_dwell_sec=if(blocked_q==1 AND prev_blk==1 AND isnotnull(prev_ts), if(_time-prev_ts>0,_time-prev_ts,0), if(blocked_q==1,0,0))
| eventstats sum(eval(if(lane=="restarts",restart_window,0))) AS cluster_restart_hint sum(eval(if(lane=="tcpin",fwd_cnt,0))) AS cluster_tcpin_sum max(eval(if(lane=="q_fill",fill_ratio,0))) AS cluster_peak_fill BY cluster
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen
        max(eval(if(lane=="q_fill",fill_ratio,null()))) AS fill_ratio
        max(eval(if(lane=="q_fill",blocked_dwell_sec,null()))) AS blocked_dwell_sec
        max(eval(if(lane=="q_thru",kbps_in,null()))) AS kbps_in
        max(eval(if(lane=="q_thru",kbps_out,null()))) AS kbps_out
        max(eval(if(lane=="tcpin",fwd_cnt,null()))) AS tcpin_connections
        max(eval(if(lane=="pl_chan",bytes_in,null()))) AS pipeline_chan_kb
        max(eval(if(lane=="restarts",restart_window,null()))) AS restart_hint
        values(eval(if(lane=="q_fill",qname,null()))) AS qname_vals
        values(lane) AS lane_vals
  BY cluster host
| eval worst_queue=mvindex(mvdedup(qname_vals),0)
| eval source_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lane_vals),0)
| eval burn_rate=if(kbps_out>0 AND fill_ratio>0, round(kbps_out/(fill_ratio+0.0001),2), null())
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(fill_ratio,0)>=0.92 AND coalesce(restart_hint,0)<1, "page",
    coalesce(blocked_dwell_sec,0)>300 AND coalesce(restart_hint,0)<1 AND coalesce(fill_ratio,0)>=0.85, "page",
    coalesce(fill_ratio,0)>=0.80, "warn",
    coalesce(tcpin_connections,0)>=5000, "warn",
    coalesce(pipeline_chan_kb,0)>=1000000, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| join type=left max=0 cluster
    [| inputlookup splunk_indexer_inventory.csv
     | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
     | dedup cluster
     | fields cluster, cluster_tier, owner_team, environment ]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier, "unrated"), owner_team=coalesce(owner_team, "unowned"), environment=coalesce(environment, "unknown")
| table cluster host worst_queue fill_ratio blocked_dwell_sec kbps_in kbps_out burn_rate tcpin_connections pipeline_chan_kb restart_hint severity last_seen source_lane cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[uc_13_1_1_indexer_queue_saturation_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch uc_13_1_1_indexer_queue_saturation | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance notes: if Job Inspector shows multisearch skew, materialize per-host queue peaks into an hourly summary index keyed on cluster and host, widen the alert to earliest=-2h@h on the summary, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap parallel subsearch fan-out on tiny search heads.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the indexer under study. Open metrics.log on the peer and confirm group=queue lines arrive for parsingQueue, aggQueue, typingQueue, and indexQueue with non-null size fields. Compare manual tail timestamps to Splunk _time to catch clock skew or ingestion delay.

Use the Monitoring Console queue and indexing dashboards as a second opinion: peaks should align between MC visuals and the uc_13_1_1_indexer_queue_saturation table within one sampling interval. When they diverge, verify that the MC search head can read all peers and that SSL or firewall changes did not partition a site.

Run btool on props and transforms for sourcetype=splunkd to confirm LINE_BREAKER and TRUNCATE settings still allow full metrics.log lines; truncated JSON fragments produce false calm when queue names disappear.

Inspect indexer.log around the same window for WARN or ERROR lines about queue blockage, pipeline failures, or thruput stalls. Pair with iostat or storage latency checks from Splunk_TA_nix when indexQueue suspicion focuses on disk.

Deliberate restart test: schedule a maintenance window on a non-production peer, capture restart_hint in the search, and confirm severity suppresses page-tier while fill briefly rises then drains. If pages still fire, tighten restart matching in the _audit arm or extend the restart_hint window.

Negative test: on a calm cluster with low tcpin fan-in, verify warn and page rows empty when thresholds are temporarily raised in a clone, then restore production thresholds.

Job Inspector: confirm scan counts remain proportional to _internal volume; unexpected explosion suggests unbounded wildcards in supporting macros.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — parsingQueue saturated with long blocked dwell: investigate props.conf REGEX and SEDCMD explosions, malformed timestamps forcing strptime retries, and sudden sourcetype fan-out. Use the Monitoring Console parsing warnings and compare recent transforms deployments.

Case 2 — aggQueue saturated: examine LINE_BREAKER, TRUNCATE, SHOULD_LINEMERGE, and BREAK_ONLY_BEFORE settings; huge multiline events inflate aggregation cost. Validate that a well-meaning universal TA change did not triple event size.

Case 3 — typingQueue saturated: focus on transforms.conf heavy regex, LOOKUP-heavy pipelines, and computed fields evaluated per event. Diff recent cluster bundle pushes that added new extractions.

Case 4 — indexQueue saturated with healthy tcpin: prioritize disk latency, RAID health, SmartStore object store stalls, and antivirus interference on hot volumes. Pair with UC-13.1.23 context before blaming parsers.

Case 5 — tcpin connection storm: map forwarder mass-restarts, network blips, or load balancer churn; check for deployment server storms and SSL handshake failures causing reconnect loops.

Case 6 — bundle push throughput dip: correlate time windows with search head cluster bundle replication jobs; expect short-lived typing pressure while new knowledge loads.

Case 7 — post-restart healthy backlog: when restart_hint is positive, treat rising fill as expected until dwell timers exceed policy; document minutes-to-green service levels to prevent duplicate tickets.

Case 8 — hot bucket roll dip: momentary indexQueue spikes during bucket rolls or coldToFrozen transitions can look like saturation; verify bucket paths and storage policy changes before paging application teams.

Case 9 — summary indexing or report acceleration burst: elevated aggQueue and typingQueue with stable forwarder counts may reflect scheduled summaries; cross-check saved search schedules.

Case 10 — source-to-source compression mismatch: when forwarders negotiate compression changes, tcpin counts can oscillate; validate outputs.conf and indexer receiving settings alongside queue trends.

Case 11 — indexer reboot mid-window: hardware maintenance or hypervisor live migration may truncate metrics streams; require second-source confirmation from MC before executive escalation.

Case 12 — false calm after sourcetype mass-reroute: if inputs.conf changes steer huge volumes to new queues while old sourcetype filters stop matching, metrics may look quiet briefly while tcpin and disk tell a different story; reconcile inputs and transforms against the deployment server.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how full the inner processing lines get on the machines that accept and store logs, and we measure how long they stay stuck that way after big restarts or traffic spikes. When something is truly backed up—not just busy for a minute—we raise a clear signal so the right team fixes it.
hardware: Applies to Splunk Enterprise indexers on Linux or supported UNIX peers running splunkd with metrics.log enabled, including clustered indexers, standalone indexers, and SmartStore-backed peers where queue pressure still reflects local pipeline health.
